tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated creatinine and proteinuria, suggesting impaired renal function. Urine is cloudy, brown, with low specific gravity, and contains glucose, ketones, and bilirubin, indicating possible tubular dysfunction or glomerular disease. Chloride is mildly elevated. LDL cholesterol is high, increasing cardiovascular risk. HbA1c is low, possibly due to anemia or recent changes in glucose control. Recommend nephrology evaluation and further assessment for underlying renal and metabolic disorders.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Nonproliferative retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
